steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The LNH predicts a negative correlation between exotericity and grammatical complexity (fusion and informativity). To be inconsistent with the LNH, the data must show the opposite—a positive or non-negative correlation between exotericity and those complexity indices.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Shcherbakova and colleagues' analysis showed a slightly positive correlation between grammatical complexity and fusion.: describes a finding that does not compare exotericity with fusion or informativity—does not address the LNH prediction. ✗
  • Choice B — Shcherbakova and colleagues' analysis showed a slightly negative correlation between grammatical complexity and exotericity.: describes a negative correlation consistent with what the LNH predicts. ✗
  • Choice C — Shcherbakova and colleagues' analysis showed a slightly positive correlation between fusion and exotericity and between informativity and exotericity.: reports a slightly positive correlation between fusion and exotericity and between informativity and exotericity—directly contradicting the LNH's predicted inverse relationship. ✓
  • Choice D — Shcherbakova and colleagues' analysis showed a slightly negative correlation between grammatical complexity and fusion and between grammatical complexity and informativity.: describes correlations among complexity indices themselves, not between exotericity and complexity. ✗
C is correct.
